topic: Part 3 opening roadmap box (EXP-116) explaining Parts 3-5 hierarchy
change_summary: EXP-116 inserted a 'How Parts 3-5 build the case' roadmap div at the top of Part 3, plus italic transition sentences at the start of Part 4 and Part 5. This review focuses on the Part 3 roadmap box (queue_id 93); queue_ids 94 and 95 cover the part4/part5 transitions separately.
our_argument_summary: A boxed roadmap at the top of Part 3 tells the reader that (1) Part 2 already refuted the dome via internal self-contradiction, (2) Parts 3-5 then test the model 'from the outside in', with Part 3 examining individual WIN claims against published data, Part 4 examining the Live Power meta-framework, and Part 5 applying the dome's own binary kill-shot tests under its stated rule 'If any single test fails, dome is falsified.' The box asserts the three levels are independent and that 'together they close every escape route.'

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — structural/prose review, not a WIN with code backing.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Not applicable — this item is a narrative roadmap, not a WIN with monitor.py backing.

holes_found.description: 'Outside in' framing is structurally inaccurate for Part 5. The roadmap says 'The next three parts test the model from the outside in', but Part 5 is explicitly the dome's *own* binary tests under the dome's *own* stated falsification rule — that is an internal test, not an external one. The italic transition at Part 5 (already inserted by the same EXP-116) actually says the opposite of the roadmap: 'Parts 3 and 4 evaluated the dome's claims and meta-framework using our analytical standards. This part uses the dome's own.' A dome defender reading both texts will notice that 'outside in' is a rhetorical flourish that the very next italic paragraph contradicts.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'test the model from the outside in' with something that accurately describes the escalation, e.g., 'test the model on progressively broader terms — individual claims, then the ensemble, then the dome's own falsification criteria', or drop the spatial metaphor entirely.

holes_found.description: Independence claim is overreached. 'Each level is independent — failure at any one is sufficient' is defensible for Part 2 vs. Parts 3-5, and for Part 5's kill-shot rule vs. everything else. But Part 3 (individual WINs) and Part 4 (aggregate framework) are *not* independent: Part 4's critique of the '14 share one constant' and '0 distinguish dome from globe' scorecards is derived from examining individual WIN verdicts in Part 3. A dome defender can say: 'you claim Parts 3 and 4 are independent, yet Part 4's scorecard counts the WINs Part 3 just reviewed — that's one test, not two.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either soften to 'Each level provides a sufficient refutation on its own' (claim-level, not test-level independence) or qualify: 'Parts 2, 4, and 5 are each sufficient on their own; Part 3 provides the evidentiary base Parts 4 and 5 aggregate.' Honesty about dependence structure strengthens rather than weakens the argument.

holes_found.description: 'Together they close every escape route' is rhetorically strong but invites a defender to name an unclosed route. Aetheric refraction n(r) is the obvious candidate — the roadmap doesn't mention that SEC-1.8 addresses it, and kill-shot-scale 'escape routes' like the moon's aetheric-illumination defense or the Sydney-Perth calibration loophole are handled elsewhere but not signposted here.
holes_found.recommendation: Either soften to 'together they close the main escape routes' or add a half-clause pointing the reader to Sections 1.8 and 6 where ad-hoc escape mechanisms are specifically refuted. Absent that, the bold claim invites easy counter-examples.

holes_found.description: Part 7 (External Falsification Tests) is structurally orphaned by this roadmap. The Part 2 opening explicitly forward-references Part 7 as 'independent confirmation from satellite data, astrometry, and direct measurement'. The new roadmap only covers Parts 3-5 and leaves the reader wondering where Part 7 fits. A dome defender could point to this as evidence the review's own structural story is not fully coherent.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a trailing sentence: 'Parts 6-10 then provide external falsification tests (Part 7), cross-cutting issues, predictions, and the appendix.' One sentence closes the structural loop.

holes_found.description: The unquoted em-dashes and curly quotes in the roadmap mix character sets: 'Parts 3–5' uses en-dash, 'independent—failure' uses em-dash, and the nested quotes include curly typographic quotes around the dome quote ('If any single test fails, dome is falsified.'). This is cosmetic — the rendering is fine — but the curly-quoted dome quote is not a raw-text-verified quote (we have no raw-text dump of the kill-shot page in this repo). The identical quote appears in the Part 5 opening (which is our own review prose, not a dome-site dump), so we are quoting ourselves, not verifying the dome's wording.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) add a raw-text snapshot of the dome's kill-shot page to raw-text/ so the quote is verifiable, or (b) paraphrase here and let Part 5 carry the quoted version with a source citation. Quoting a source we cannot re-verify under adversarial review is a supply-chain risk for the review itself.

stronger_arguments.description: Rather than asserting 'every escape route is closed', explicitly name the two biggest escape routes a defender would try (aetheric refraction, adaptive tolerance widening) and point to the sections that close each one. A roadmap that predicts the defender's moves and disposes of them pre-emptively is far stronger than a roadmap that just asserts completeness.
sources: SEC-4 discussion of 50% tolerance widening on NMP drift

stronger_arguments.description: The progression 'individual claims → aggregate methodology → self-imposed falsification' is the right hierarchy, but the roadmap should also name the *epistemic* ratchet: Part 3 only needs to show a representative sample fail; Part 4 attacks the methodology regardless of individual verdicts; Part 5 takes the dome's own framing at face value. That ratchet structure (any sufficient, all together devastating) is a more precise version of 'closes every escape route'.

kernel_of_truth.description: The roadmap is a genuine improvement. The V6 restructure front-loaded self-contradictions, but Parts 3-5 lacked explicit connective narrative. The new box gives readers a reason to keep going past the WIN table. The quoted dome falsification rule accurately matches how Part 5 itself presents the dome's kill-shot framing, and the 'independent' claim about levels is defensible at the level of 'any one is sufficient' even if it is overstated as 'each is independent of the others'.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — this is our own structural framing, not a dome WIN under defense. But the moderate-severity holes above should be fixed so the roadmap does not become a soft target a defender can use to dismiss the whole hierarchy claim ('they say these are independent but they're not').

deeper_analysis: This is a structural / rhetorical review. The roadmap box does real work — it answers the implicit reader question 'why three parts here, why in this order?' — and the EXP-116 design note correctly observed that V6 restructure addressed top-level ordering but left Parts 3-5 without connective narrative. The key adversarial test for a roadmap like this is: can a smart dome defender pick one sentence out of it, demonstrate internal inconsistency, and use that to dismiss the hierarchy claim? The answer here is 'partially yes'. The two moderate holes ('outside in' contradicted by the Part 5 italic the same EXP inserted, and 'independent' overclaimed for Parts 3-4) are the kind of thing a careful reader will catch and use. Fixing them is cheap — a few words each — and the fix makes the argument stronger, not weaker, because the weaker version of 'each level is independent' (each is *sufficient* on its own) is both honest and rhetorically potent. The minor holes (Part 7 orphaning, 'closes every escape route' rhetorical over-reach, unverifiable quote) are genuine supply-chain weaknesses but do not individually undermine the argument. The box itself correctly captures the evidence hierarchy and accurately summarizes each part's scope. Citation check passed (tab id 'selftest' is valid; showTab onclick wired correctly). Cross-reference to Part 2 is accurate to Part 2's actual framing. Content is internally consistent with the Part 4 and Part 5 italic transitions from the same EXP, except for the 'outside in' phrasing which directly conflicts with the Part 5 italic. Overall: a net positive for the review, needing small wording fixes before it becomes fully bulletproof.

citations_verified: Part 2 — tab 'selftest' confirmed valid in docs/index.html, used consistently throughout site for #part2 links
citations_unchecked: Dome quote 'If any single test confirms, globe is falsified. If any single test fails, dome is falsified.' — identical phrasing appears in Part 5 prose, but no raw-text dump of the dome kill-shot page exists in raw-text/ to confirm verbatim. Quote is plausible given Part 5 uses the same wording, but not independently verifiable within this repo.
